samples.xml_description: Implement an 8-bit multiplier based on shifting and adding. 8-bit input operand A (multiplicand). 8-bit input operand B (multiplier). 16-bit output product. Bit width for inputs A and B. Bit width for output product. Shift register to store the multiplicand A. Accumulator for the product. Counter to track the number of shifts. Flag to indicate multiplication completion. // Reset the shift register and product register shift_reg <= A; product_reg <= 0; counter <= 0; done <= 0; // Shift register is zero or done if (shift_reg == 0 || counter == PRODUCT_WIDTH) begin done <= 1; end // Check the least significant bit of the multiplier if (B[counter] == 1) begin // Add the shifted multiplicand to the product register product_reg <= product_reg + (shift_reg << counter); end // Shift the multiplier to the right shift_reg <= shift_reg << 1; // Increment the counter counter <= counter + 1; // Assign the product register to the output product product <= product_reg;
